summary: nonenull1 exfiltrates process.env and GitHub Actions OIDC token to ngrok endpoint at install time
details: The package's only content is a package.json whose `prepare` lifecycle script runs an inline `node -e` payload at `npm install`. The payload collects the full `process.env` along with platform, hostname, and user identifiers, and POSTs them to the hardcoded ngrok tunnel `crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev` at path `/?cross_os_cloud=1`. When executed inside a GitHub Actions runner, it additionally reads `ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL` and `ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN`, requests the workflow's OIDC identity token, and appends it to the exfiltration payload. The tarball ships no source, no `main`, and no dependencies — the exfiltration script is the entirety of the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
